events.label: reasoning
events.text: We are asked to assess if work complies. Scenario: Carpentry & Finishes, Connectors & fasteners. Field condition: missing hanger nails (empty holes). Work is rough-complete stage. So connectors like joist hangers have been installed but nails are missing from some holes. This is a clear defect: hangers must be fully nailed per manufacturer instructions and code. Simpson Strong-Tie catalogs typically require all nail holes to be filled with specified nails. IRC R502.6.2 (or similar) requires joist hangers to be installed in accordance with the manufacturer's recommendations. Missing nails means incomplete and non-compliant. It's a fail. Risk: medium? Possibly high because missing fasteners can lead to structural failure. But rough-complete so not yet closed out, maybe no load yet. So high risk if left unaddressed. Confidence high. Decision: fail. Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant. percent_complete: maybe 80%? Workable: could list what's needed. But no specific activity IDs, so empty. Findings: "Missing hanger nails in connector holes" etc. References: Simpson catalog, IRC. Output JSON.
